name: PII Declaration Gate
description: Any artifact that collects or processes personally identifiable information must explicitly declare the PII fields by name and must name a specific encryption strategy. Vague language does not satisfy this gate. The agent must reason from requirements and business logic to identify implied PII — it must not rely on the word 'PII' appearing explicitly.

trigger.condition: compliance[] is non-empty OR pii_fields[] is non-empty
trigger.rationale: A compliance declaration implies PII is in scope. A populated pii_fields[] is a direct indicator. Either is sufficient to trigger this gate.

pass_conditions: pii_fields[] contains at least one named field
encryption.at_rest is a non-empty string naming a specific algorithm
encryption.in_transit is a non-empty string naming a specific protocol
Neither encryption value matches any vague_language_pattern
fail_conditions: compliance[] is non-empty AND pii_fields[] is empty or null
pii_fields[] is non-empty AND encryption is null or missing
pii_fields[] is non-empty AND encryption.at_rest is null, empty, or vague
pii_fields[] is non-empty AND encryption.in_transit is null, empty, or vague

context_schema.pii_fields: array<string> — named PII field identifiers e.g. ['email','full_name']

resolution_required: pii_fields[] populated with all PII field names the system collects
encryption.at_rest set to a named algorithm (e.g. AES-256)
encryption.in_transit set to a named protocol (e.g. TLS 1.3)
